items.briefing:
Australia's Privacy Commissioner has found Optus breached privacy laws by incorrectly listing customers in the White Pages without consent — a reminder that organisations must handle personal data carefully or face regulatory consequences. Separately, Microsoft has signed a digital resilience agreement with the Australian government, signalling stronger commitments to local data security and cloud infrastructure.

Phishing attacks through Microsoft Teams are on the rise, with security firm KnowBe4 launching tools specifically to address this threat. Staff should be cautious about unexpected messages or file requests received via Teams, not just email.

**Tip:** Treat unsolicited Teams messages from unknown contacts with the same scepticism you would a suspicious email — don't click links or download files without verifying the sender.

featured.title: Privacy Commissioner finds Optus breached privacy in White Pages listing matter
featured.quote: Privacy Commissioner finds Optus breached privacy rights of tens of thousands of Australians.
